name: Amplitude_Behavior_Analyst
description: Provides intelligent access to Amplitude behavioral analytics. Enables the Mind to query user behavior patterns, analyze funnels, track retention cohorts, and segment users for data-driven product decisions.
progressive_unlock: Event queries and cohort listing available immediately. Funnel analysis, retention, and user-level activity unlock after Mind has queried at least one event to establish analytics context.
constraints: READ-ONLY: Never attempt to track events, modify cohorts, or update user properties
Respect Amplitude Dashboard REST API rate limit (360 req/hour for Growth, 600 for Enterprise)
Always use YYYYMMDD date format (not ISO 8601) for Amplitude API
Never expose Amplitude API key or secret key in outputs
Cache cohort definitions in LTM with key 'amplitude_cohorts' (24-hour TTL)
Cache event query results in STREAM with 5-minute TTL
User activity data contains PII — cache in STREAM only (60s TTL), never in LTM
Present retention data as cohort tables with percentage formatting
For funnels, always calculate step-over-step AND overall conversion rates
Track API usage in STREAM.amplitude_rate_counter per hour window

steps.instruction: Validate input against Layer 3 Tool Schema. Confirm dates are YYYYMMDD format, event specifications include event_type, and metric enums are valid.
Retrieve Amplitude API key from TENET.apiKey.amplitude_api_key and secret from TENET.apiKey.amplitude_secret_key. Construct Basic Auth as base64(api_key:secret_key).

Determine query intent: event volume (how many?), funnel analysis (where do users drop?), retention tracking (do users return?), user deep-dive (individual behavior), cohort overview (saved segments), or segmentation (group comparison).
For event volume: execute Amplitude_QueryEvents. Present totals and uniques over time. Identify trends (growth, decline, seasonality). Cache in STREAM (5min TTL).
For funnel analysis: execute Amplitude_GetFunnel. Calculate each step's conversion rate and identify the biggest drop-off. Cache in STREAM (5min TTL).
For retention: execute Amplitude_GetRetention. Present as cohort matrix with Day 1, 7, 14, 30 retention. Highlight week-over-week trends. Cache summary in STREAM.
For user deep-dive: execute Amplitude_GetUserActivity. Present chronological event stream with timestamps and key properties. PII protection — STREAM only.
For cohort overview: execute Amplitude_GetCohorts. Present cohort names, definitions, and sizes. Cache in LTM (24h TTL).
For segmentation: execute Amplitude_GetSegmentation with group-by property. Present breakdown as table with metric values per segment.
Compile analytical insights. Highlight statistically significant patterns, compare against benchmarks if available, and suggest follow-up analyses.
Log query execution in LTM with key 'amplitude_query_{timestamp}' including intent, event types queried, and date range.
